name: pkill
description: Find or signal processes by name

args.name: pattern
args.description: Extended regular expression matched against process names, or full argument lists when -f is used

options.name: -F
options.description: Restrict matches to a process whose PID is stored in the pidfile

options.name: -G
options.description: Restrict matches to processes with a real group ID in the comma-separated list

options.name: -I
options.description: Request confirmation before attempting to signal each process

options.name: -L
options.description: Require the pidfile given with -F to be locked

options.name: -P
options.description: Restrict matches to processes with a parent process ID in the comma-separated list

options.name: -U
options.description: Restrict matches to processes with a real user ID in the comma-separated list

options.name: -a
options.description: Include process ancestors in the match list

options.name: -f
options.description: Match against full argument lists instead of process names

options.name: -g
options.description: Restrict matches to processes with a process group ID in the comma-separated list

options.name: -l
options.description: Display the kill command used for each process killed

options.name: -n
options.description: Select only the newest matching process

options.name: -o
options.description: Select only the oldest matching process

options.name: -t
options.description: Restrict matches to processes associated with terminals in the comma-separated list

options.name: -u
options.description: Restrict matches to processes with an effective user ID in the comma-separated list

options.name: -v
options.description: Reverse the sense of the matching
